purpose: Record the container rebuild fix and the first corrected-topology MTP1 4xMI350 A/B between standard EP and MORI native compact on the current source tree.

container_fix.question: Can we fix the missing RDMA/libpci headers and runtime libraries inside the containers for both MI350 nodes?
container_fix.answer: Yes. A derived dev-deps image was built on both nodes and used for the MORI rebuild and run gates.
container_fix.rebuild_result: MORI remote rebuild passed on do-sonle5-mi350-gpu; output included has_compact_combine True.
container_fix.small_smoke_result: S128 4-GPU native compact smoke passed one training step with finite loss and clean process-group teardown.

source_change_under_test.summary: When MORI native count metadata is available, avoid building/saving the full expert-major to rank-major permutation sidecar. Native combine can consume count metadata directly; the fallback permutation tensor remains required when count metadata is absent.

runs.failure: HSA memory access fault in backward, matching the known current-source Triton weighted-scatter score-gradient issue at this production shape.
runs.interpretation: Invalid as a throughput control; use the scoretorch-safe control below for this source tuple.
Fair current-source corrected-topology 4x standard EP control. It is very close to the MI350 memory ceiling at this MTP1 MBS4 attention-only checkpointing shape.
Not a throughput candidate. The low logged Torch memory at step 1 does not capture enough hidden HSA/MORI heap/workspace residency to explain the step-2 dispatch OOR.

failure.stack_hint: cross_entropy/loss while entering the next train step

runs.interpretation: Heap16 does not solve the corrected-topology fit issue. It shifts the failure location but still leaves only tens of MB free after one step.

Disabling retained released handles and forcing release-time GC did not fix the corrected MTP1 native fit issue. The blocker is not stale cross-step handle cache alone; the live native compact staging/heap/layout residency is already too large at this surface.

read.container: The container build failure is fixed by the derived dev-deps image on both nodes. The previous missing-header failure is no longer the blocker.
read.current_baseline: The corrected MTP1 4x current-source control is standard EP with scoretorch-safe weighted-scatter score gradients: 6350.4 tok/GPU/s and 314.386 TFLOPs/GPU over steps 2-6, peak logged memory 279.99 GiB.
read.native_mori: MORI native compact is graph-connected and S128-smoked, but it does not currently fit the corrected MTP1 MBS4 S4096 surface for six steps. Heap32, heap16, and release-time handle GC all fail after one finite step with HSA out-of-resources.
read.root_cause_direction: This is now a native compact live-residency/layout problem, not a missing container dependency or stale released-handle cache. The next source target is reducing MORI native hidden workspace and collapsing final expert-major/compact-return layouts, rather than more heap-size tuning, release-cache toggles, or route-mask knobs.

next_steps: Run the native compact path under low-intrusion stage timing or MORI stage profiler on a smaller fit surface to isolate hidden HSA/MORI residency.
Keep the standard EP scoretorch-safe run as the fair corrected current-source 4x control until the integrated Triton score-gradient fault is fixed.
Do not promote native MORI from one-step or three-step bursts; require at least a six-step gate at the matched shape, then a longer stability run when it beats the control.
On a clean full 8x surface, repeat only after the 4x native compact memory issue is closed.
